summary: Local code-level verification confirmed the SSRF mechanism: the default DATASET_IMPORT_ALLOWED_DATA_URLS=[r".*"] allows any URL through validate_data_uri(), and urllib.request.urlopen() fetches the attacker-specified URL. A local test started an internal HTTP server, passed its URL through the vulnerable validation + fetch chain, and confirmed the server received the request. Docker-based end-to-end PoC requires Superset image build.
expected_security_impact: The Superset server should fetch the internal URL (http://127.0.0.1:18888/secret-internal-endpoint) and the internal server should record the request, proving that DATASET_IMPORT_ALLOWED_DATA_URLS=[r".*"] allows arbitrary URL fetching from the server's network context.
actual_observed_result: Local verification confirmed: a mock internal HTTP server at 127.0.0.1:18899 received a request after the SSRF URL passed through validate_data_uri() (matched by r".*") and was fetched by urllib.request.urlopen(). Docker-based end-to-end PoC not executed due to Docker network constraints preventing image build.
verification_method: Local code-level test: (1) started internal HTTP server recording requests, (2) called validate_data_uri() with internal URL confirming r".*" allows it, (3) called urllib.request.urlopen() confirming fetch occurs, (4) verified proof file written by internal server. Docker verify.sh additionally checks target repo source for vulnerable config.
evidence_produced_by_run: Local test confirmed: DATASET_IMPORT_ALLOWED_DATA_URLS=[r".*"] allows any URL through validate_data_uri()
Local test confirmed: urllib.request.urlopen() successfully fetches internal URLs after validation
Internal server proof file confirmed: SSRF_REQUEST_RECEIVED path=/secret-metadata-endpoint
Static analysis confirms full data flow: POST /api/v1/dataset/import/ -> ImportDatasetsCommand -> load_data() -> request.urlopen(data_uri)
Alpha role permission (can_write on Dataset) confirmed in security/manager.py
limitations_or_blockers: Docker image build could not complete due to network constraints preventing apt-get and pip install
End-to-end PoC through HTTP API requires running Superset instance with database, users, and permissions configured

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned version tag, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
